summary: Tested NyaInfo landing MVP end-to-end. Backend: 11/11 pytest cases pass against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL covering health, contact CRUD, validation (422 on missing/invalid email), persistence after POST, descending sort, and Mongo _id exclusion. Frontend: landing renders without console errors, hero heading/CTAs present, navbar with all nav anchors and CTA, 6 project cards (p1..p6), 6 FAQ items (clicking expands), 3 pricing tiers (spark/studio/scale), testimonials carousel prev/next moves embla track (transform shifted from translate3d(0,0,0) to translate3d(-607.88px,0,0)), smooth-scroll on nav links works (scrollY=1080 after Services click), contact form submission shows success toast and the record persists in MongoDB (verified via GET /api/contact). Mobile menu hamburger toggles full-screen overlay with all nav links visible.

minor.issue: Single transient 502 on first attempt (passed on retry; likely cold-start through ingress)

critical_code_review_comments: server.py: `logger` is referenced inside submit_contact (line ~100) but defined below the route declarations (line ~135). This works at runtime because the route handler executes after module load, but reads as a code smell — move logging.basicConfig and logger= to the top of the file (just below imports) before any route uses it.

server.py: CORSMiddleware is added AFTER app.include_router(api_router). FastAPI applies middleware regardless of order for current versions, but convention is to add middleware before including routers for clarity.

Contact endpoint stores email as plain `str` in the Contact model (response model) while ContactCreate uses EmailStr. This is intentional to keep response simple but be aware older saved records won't be re-validated.
No upper bound on GET /api/contact `limit` query (currently capped only by Mongo to_list cap). Consider clamping to e.g. min(limit, 500) to prevent large response abuse.
Frontend Landing.jsx is fine, but if it grows, sections look great; consider lazy-loading below-the-fold sections (Testimonials, Team, Pricing) for better LCP.

Mobile menu toggle data-testid is `navbar-mobile-toggle` (works), and the slide-down panel uses `navbar-mobile-menu`. Documentation in the review request mentioned `mobile-menu-toggle` — minor naming mismatch only relevant for test scripting.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this MVP
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_ prefixed contact submissions in MongoDB `contacts` collection during testing (TEST_Playwright User, TEST_S1_*, TEST_S2_*, TEST_User_*, TEST_Min_*). Safe to leave or purge with `db.contacts.deleteMany({name: /^TEST_/})`.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_nyainfo_api.py (11 tests). Frontend testids verified: navbar-cta, navbar-mobile-toggle, navbar-mobile-menu, hero-primary-cta, project-card-p1..p6, pricing-card-{spark|studio|scale}, faq-item-0..5, testimonials-prev/next, testimonial-card-0/1, testimonials-carousel, testimonials-section, contact-input-{name,email,company,subject,message}, contact-submit-button. Note: testimonial quote does not have `[data-testid="testimonial-quote"]`; use `testimonial-card-{i}` for assertions, and verify carousel motion via embla track div.style.transform inside `[data-testid="testimonials-section"]`.
